back_translation: When you go out to war against your enemies and see horse and chariot, a people more numerous than you, you shall not fear from them, for the Lord your God is with you, who brought you up from the land of Egypt.
And it shall be, when you draw near to the battle, the priest shall come forward and speak to the people,

and he shall say to them: Hear, O Israel, you are drawing near today to the battle against your enemies. Do not let your heart be faint; do not fear and do not tremble and do not be terrified before them.
For the Lord your God is the one who goes with you, to fight for you with your enemies, to save you.

And the officers shall speak to the people, saying: Who is the man who has built a new house and has not dedicated it? Let him go and return to his house, lest he die in the battle and another man dedicate it.
And who is the man who has planted a vineyard and has not begun to use it? Let him go and return to his house, lest he die in the battle and another man begin to use it.
And who is the man who has betrothed a wife and has not taken her? Let him go and return to his house, lest he die in the battle and another man take her.

And the officers shall continue to speak to the people, and they shall say: Who is the man who is afraid and faint of heart? Let him go and return to his house, and let him not melt the heart of his brothers like his own heart.
And it shall be, when the officers have finished speaking to the people, they shall appoint commanders of armies at the head of the people.
When you draw near to a city to fight against it, you shall call to it for peace.
And it shall be, if peace it answers you and opens to you, then all the people who are found in it shall be to you for forced labor and shall serve you.
But if it does not make peace with you and makes war with you, then you shall besiege it.
And the Lord your God shall give it into your hand, and you shall strike every male of it with the edge of the sword.

Only the women and the children and the cattle, and all that shall be in the city, all its spoil, you shall plunder for yourself; and you shall eat the spoil of your enemies that the Lord your God has given to you.
Thus you shall do to all the cities very far from you, which are not of the cities of these nations here.
Only from the cities of these peoples that the Lord your God is giving to you as an inheritance, you shall not let live any breath.
For devoting to destruction you shall devote them: the Hittite and the Amorite, the Canaanite and the Perizzite, the Hivite and the Jebusite, as the Lord your God has commanded you,
so that they shall not teach you to do according to all their abominations that they have done for their gods, and you sin to the Lord your God.

When you besiege a city for many days, to fight against it to capture it, you shall not destroy its trees by swinging an axe upon them, for from them you shall eat, and them you shall not cut down; for is the man of the field a tree, that should come from before you in the siege?
Only a tree that you know that it is not a tree of food, that one you may destroy and cut down, and you shall build a siege upon the city that is waging war against you, until its falling.
